iCAN symposium talk!

Single-cell spatial proteomics identifies cell-cycle–resolved tumor niches in chemotherapy-treated ovarian cancer

Two-fold purpose:
1) FIMM coffee break (20min)
2) iCAN symposium (10 min)

Structure of the speech
Title/Presentation
Ovarian cancer background / treatment
Cell-cycle background - cohort + experiment panel
Spatial technologies + t-CycIF technology: Showcasing the staining patterns in IF
Explaining challenges for phenotyping with marker gradients
Cell-cycle phenotypes: staining patterns and pseudotime
Patient archetypes (CLINICAL RELEVANCE)
TME analysis (CLINICAL RELEVANCE)
GeoMx analysis of pRb+ Ki67+ (BIOLOGICAL RELEVANCE)

GeoMx exploration - Ki67+ pRb+ RCNs

Ki67+ niches are High-turnover, stressed cells
MCM pathway -> replication initiation
Replication stress -> DSB repair via induced replication (BIR/HR)
Protein K6-linked ubiquitination -> BRCA-mediated repair
Positive regulation of chromosome condensation

Great amount of experiments this fall

Expansion t-CycIF objectives

Main aim: Deep phenotyping and mapping of cancer cell cycle states across a larger cohort
Sub-aims:
Expanding the patient number to better understand clinical associations
Deeper phenotyping of cell cycle states (improved functional panel)
Integrating multiple data modalities 
Spatial transcriptomics: GeoMx, Xenium
WES, bulk RNA-seq
